intro_inherited: Your uncle just died. The good news? He left you everything.
A fat stack of cash, a garage with a questionable roof, and a ship that... well, it's got a hull. Mostly.
Time to hit the sector and make a name for yourself.
robbed: You pull up to the strip with your airlock open and your wallet out like an absolute tourist.
Three crews you've never seen before box you in. It's over before your reactor stops ticking.
They took everything. Every last credit. Even your good sunglasses.
You limp home on fumes with nothing but your dignity. Actually, they took that too.
junkyard: On the walk of shame home, you pass a salvage yard. The fence has a hole. You have nothing to lose.
After an hour of tetanus-risk digging, you drag out some parts that almost look functional.
It's not much, but it's yours. Well, it's the salvage yard's. But they won't miss it.
teach_inventory: You've got parts now. Questionable parts, but parts.
Use `/inventory` to see what you dragged out of that dumpster. You can tap any part in the list to inspect it.
teach_inspect: Not bad. Not good either, but not bad.
Want a closer look? Use `/inspect {card_name}` to check the stats. Each copy rolls slightly different — some are better than others.
teach_equip: Knowledge is power. Unfortunately, power is also power, and yours is currently sitting on the floor.
Use `/equip` to bolt these parts onto your ship. You need all **7 slots** filled before you can mint your Ship Title.
Slots to fill: **Reactor, Drive, Thrusters, Hull, Stabilizers, Retros, Overdrive**.
Or skip the grease work — `/autoequip best` will install your best parts automatically.
teach_build_preview: All 7 slots bolted. Your ship is starting to look like something.
Before you make it official, run `/build preview` — it shows which class your build is trending toward.
Class determines what races you're eligible for. It's not a choice — it comes from your stats.
teach_build_mint: See that class breakdown? That's your parts telling you what kind of ship this is.
When you're ready to lock it in, use `/build mint` to issue your **Ship Title**.
The title seals your **Reactor** and **Hull** — those are the core of the build. Swap thrusters, retros, stabilizers, and overdrive freely. Touch the core and you void the title.
teach_garage: Your ship has a name now. And a serial number. That's more than most things in this salvage yard.
Use `/hangar` to see your full build, class, and title details.
Want to scout the competition? `/peek @player` lets you see anyone's build.
teach_race: She runs. She stops. She turns. That's three whole features.
Remember that strip where you got jumped? Time to go back.
Use `/race @player` to send a challenge. They'll have to accept before you line up.
Most races are gated by **class** — Sprint is open to everyone, but Endurance and Gauntlet events require the right build. Your class is set by your stats, not your choice.
Some events go further and lock to a specific **hull class** too — Gauntlet Hauler only, Sprint Scout only, that kind of thing. You'll know when you try to enter and can't.
Feeling bold? Add a wager — `/race @player wager:50` puts Creds on the line.
race_win: HOLY SHIT. You actually won. In THAT thing.
Your opponent is staring at their nav console trying to figure out what just happened.
Word on the sector travels fast. Someone tosses you a crate as a "welcome to the scene" gift.
race_lose: You lost. Obviously. Look at your ship.
But hey, you finished. That's more than your ship's warranty would have predicted.
Some old scrapper in the crowd saw your effort and tosses you a crate. "Everyone starts somewhere, kid."
teach_pack: A free **Salvage Crate**. Inside could be anything. Probably junk, but maybe slightly better junk.
It's already been opened for you. Let's see what you got...
teach_daily: One more thing. Every day you can use `/daily` to collect **100 Creds** and a **full set of parts** — one for every slot.
The rarity is random. Mostly junk, but every once in a while you pull something nasty.
outro: That's the basics. Build your ship, mint your title, race the sector, trade cards, and try not to wreck.
Upgrade your ship — swap wear parts (thrusters, retros, stabilizers, overdrive) any time. Replace your reactor or hull and you'll need to `/build disassemble` and re-mint.
The `/market` has parts for sale. `/trade` if you want to deal with other players directly.
Use `/multirace` to host a bigger event. Parts degrade each race, so keep an eye on your build.
Good luck out there. You're gonna need it.
